excerpt: 21   It is said that each tithi, lunar day and each of the fifteen manifestation of the Goddess Herself has two aspects: Prakashamsa, which rules the day portion of the tithi, and Vimarshamsa, which rules the night portion of the tithi. At night the Goddess in one of Her fifteen forms collects the nectar reflecting some of its qualities, while during the day She releases it, so it can recharge us. During the night nectar is replenished by Soma or the Moon, while during the day the nectar is burned or consumed by Agni or Surya. Being so, the main quality of the Moon and moonlight is to make things grow (rohana shakti of Rohini nakshatra), while the main qualitiy of the Sun is to burn things (dahana shakti of Krittika nakshatra).
